'''Bpfassh''' was an arid [[Double planet/Legends|double planet]] in the [[Sluis sector/Legends|Sluis sector]] [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] by a complicated system of [[Moon/Legends|moons]], located near [[Praesitlyn/Legends|Praesitlyn]] and [[Sluis Van/Legends|Sluis Van]]. It was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Bpfasshi/Legends|Bpfasshi]].

==Description==
The main body of the [[Bpfassh system/Legends|Bpfassh system]] in the [[Sluis sector/Legends|Sluis sector]], Bpfassh was a [[Double planet/Legends|double planet]] orbited by a complicated system of [[Moon/Legends|moons]]. The twin planets took the name of the [[Star system/Legends|system's]] [[Star/Legends|star]], [[Bpfassh (star)|Bpfassh]], and were neighbored by two systems with names unpronounceable to [[Human/Legends|Humans]]. Both were [[Temperature/Legends|temperate]] worlds with [[Atmosphere/Legends|Type II atmospheres]], a dry [[Hydrosphere/Legends|hydrosphere]], and standard [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]]. The Bpfassh worlds had barren terrain, and were home to a native population of 50 million [[Bpfasshi]], who reached a space-age technology level. The double worlds were used for the mining of natural resources, and exported [[Metal/Legends|metals]] and other resources while importing high- and mid-level technologies.<ref name="HttES" />

==History==
Bpfassh was the site of a [[Jedi Praxeum]]. Near the start of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], [[Nejaa Halcyon]] chased [[Zozridor Slayke]]'s ''[[Scarlet Thranta]]'' there in his [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the ''[[Plooriod Bodkin]]''. Slayke [[Mission to Bpfassh (Separatist Crisis)|disabled]] the ''Thranta'' and stole the ''Bodkin'' at the price of only one of his men, leaving Halcyon and his crew stranded on Bpfassh for months, disgracing him in the eyes of many.

Bpfassh's Praxeum was the site of a [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] uprising later during the Clone Wars. The Bpfasshi Dark Jedi were responsible for a rampage of terror and destruction throughout the [[Sector/Legends|sector]] before the insurrection was quelled by a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] task force led by [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]]. The Bpfasshi held a strong dislike for all Jedi since that episode. This uprising would be distorted by legend; some versions placing [[Jorus C'baoth]] on the front line, a Jedi Master who had died several years before the event.<ref name="The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref>

[[File:Bpfassh_surface.jpg|thumb|left|150px|[[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] and [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] inspect a crater after a planetary bombardment on Bpfassh.]]

Decades later, [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] [[Attack on Bpfassh|feinted]] at this system to draw ships away from [[Sluis Van/Legends|Sluis Van]]. [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] and [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] were later [[Mission to Bpfassh|attacked]] by Grand Admiral Thrawn's [[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]] here.

{{Planet-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
[[Jorus C'baoth]] was originally set as the Jedi Master who defeated the Dark Jedi; however, after the date of the Clone Wars became clear, this became impossible. ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' reconciled this by stating that legend had distorted truth.

The PC game [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']] mistakenly places the Sluis sector and, consequently, Bpfassh, in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Galactic Core]].

The planet's name is pronounced as "Bip-fash."<ref name="heir">''[[Heir to the Empire]]'' audio edition</ref>
